title: Browserbase argues agents need a code surface, not dozens of tools
friendlyTitle: AI agents may work better with code than a huge toolbox
summary: Browserbase proposes exposing a small set of typed capabilities inside a secure execution environment and letting an agent write ordinary code to combine them. Its worked example estimates about 88% less context than loading and calling a large collection of bespoke tools, while keeping filtering, retries and branching deterministic.
friendlySummary: Browserbase says an agent can write small programs to join several safe capabilities instead of learning many separate tool commands.
whyItMatters: Code mode can reduce tool-schema overhead and let agents process large results before they enter model context. It also expands what generated code can do, so the useful pattern is a constrained runtime with scoped capabilities, resource limits, logging and approval around consequential actions.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This can save context and make workflows clearer, but the code must run inside a strongly limited and monitored environment.
